pattern: Because the expression only ever adds two of the three chosen values and subtracts the third, the best choice always pairs the two largest values as the addends and the single smallest value as the one being subtracted.
hints: The expression treats two of the three chosen numbers identically (both added) and singles out the third for subtraction, so the roles, not the specific indices, are what matter.
To maximize a sum of two numbers minus a third, you always want the two added numbers as large as possible and the subtracted number as small as possible.
Sort the array (or just track the two largest and the smallest in one pass): the answer is the largest plus the second largest, minus the smallest.

edge_inputs.description: all values at the minimum allowed bound
all values at the maximum allowed bound
the smallest value is a large negative number, which greatly increases the result once subtracted
the two largest values include a duplicate that must be usable from distinct indices
negative and positive values mixed with the maximum not at either end of the array

stress.description: random values across the full constraint range, larger arrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
